items.caption: A Day on the Water — June 13, 2026
items.transcript: A clear June morning on the Oakland estuary. The fleet waits at the docks. We gather at the yacht club for the morning briefing, life vests on and ready to go. All hands turn to, readying the boats for the water. Sails come out, lines are sorted, and everything is checked twice. The mainsail climbs the mast, and the boat comes alive. Smiles all around — this is what we came for. One by one, we step aboard and find our places. Out on the water, a young sailor takes the helm, the instructor right alongside. Hands on the tiller, eyes on the sails, sailing the boat. Around us, the bay is alive with boats under sail. We glide down the estuary under a wide blue sky. A fine day on the water, harnessing the wind — the whole point of BlindSail.

items.caption: Gathering at the club for the morning briefing before we head to the boats.
items.transcript: (Automated transcription — may contain errors.) Two blind sailing our June sailing. We have a couple new Sailors here. We're going to do introductions of our Sailors first, and then we'll get to our volunteers. All right? So if you haven't sailed with us before, it's great, if you can say your name and if you've ever been sailing before and where you're from, or where you live, all right, so Joe want to start us out? Go look at San Francisco. Yeah. All right and everybody please Pick up really loud. Okay, Priscilla. I'm Krista.

items.alt: View from the Oakland Yacht Club patio: sailboats moored in a marina under a clear blue sky, with a hedge and patio chair in the foreground and the Oakland hills beyond.
items.caption: Morning at Oakland Yacht Club — the fleet waits at the docks while we gather for the day.

items.alt: A marina channel of calm water flanked by moored sailboats on both sides, with hills across the estuary under a clear sky.
items.caption: Still water and a clear sky — a perfect morning to head out on the bay.

items.alt: Marina view from a hedged patio, looking down a channel of moored sailboats toward the far shore.
items.caption: Looking out over the docks before we cast off.

items.alt: Several people in life vests prepare a sailboat named Dream Catcher at the dock, handling rigging on deck.
items.caption: All hands on Dream Catcher — checking rigging and getting her ready to sail.
Stepping aboard, gear in hand, ready to cast off.
items.transcript: No spoken dialogue in this clip; ambient marina sound only.

items.caption: Rigging up at the dock — sorting sails and getting her ready to go.
items.transcript: (Automated transcription — may contain errors.) And watch out of there. You want to go for a week? Oh my gosh.

items.alt: Sailors in life vests on the deck of a J/24 sailboat at the dock, one smiling in a blue vest and sun hat in the foreground.
items.caption: Crew aboard the J/24, all smiles as we rig up and prep to leave the dock.
Hoisting sail on the J/24 as we finish prepping to leave the slip.

items.caption: At the helm with the instructor alongside — steering us across the water.

items.alt: A young sailor in a yellow life vest steers a sailboat with a hand on the tiller, an instructor seated alongside, marina in the background.
items.caption: Hands on the tiller — taking the helm with the instructor close by.

items.alt: A young sailor steers a sailboat while a crew member sits in the foreground, out on open water with life vests on.
items.caption: At the helm on open water — steering us along under a bright sky.

items.alt: A young sailor at the helm of a sailboat under a clear sky, a crew member in sunglasses in the foreground.
items.caption: Steering steady — eyes up, hand on the tiller, sailing the boat.

items.alt: A sailboat named Bewitched sails through the estuary past moored boats and houseboats, seen from the deck of another boat.
items.caption: Bewitched glides past the houseboats — a fine sight out on the water.

items.alt: A sailboat sails through the estuary near houseboats, viewed over the side deck and lifelines of another sailboat.
items.caption: Underway on the estuary, keeping company with a boat under sail nearby.
Sailing the estuary, another boat under sail off our bow.
